task_completion.description: LibreCAD is open and the required layers are created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD interface is visible. The Layer List panel shows a 'PROFILE' layer (set to White/Color 7) and a 'BORE' layer (set to Cyan/Color 4).

task_completion.description: The base circle and nose circle for the cam profile are drawn.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A large circle (base) centered at the origin and a smaller circle (nose) positioned directly above it are visible in the drawing area.

task_completion.description: Tangent lines connecting the base and nose circles are drawn.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two straight lines connect the left and right sides of the large base circle to the smaller nose circle, forming a pear-like shape.

task_completion.description: The PROFILE shape is trimmed into a single continuous outline.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The inner overlapping segments of the base and nose circles are removed. The outer boundary of the pear shape is a single continuous loop with no internal crossing lines.

task_completion.description: The central shaft hole and keyway notch are drawn on the BORE layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A smaller circle is drawn in the center of the base circle, with a small rectangular cutout (keyway) at its top edge. These elements should be drawn in cyan.

task_completion.description: The BORE shape is trimmed into a continuous hole and the file is saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The top segment of the bore circle inside the keyway is removed so the hole and notch form one continuous shape. The LibreCAD window title or command line indicates the file is saved as 'cam_profile.dxf'.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with LibreCAD's toolbars, menus, and drawing area, rather than writing raw DXF code in a text editor.

integrity.description: The drawing evolves sequentially over time.
integrity.visual_evidence: Intermediate steps (e.g., untrimmed circles, construction lines, layer switching) are visible across the screenshots, proving the shape was constructed manually.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the task by copying a pre-made file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows do not show commands like 'wget', 'curl', or 'cp' being used to place a pre-existing 'cam_profile.dxf' directly into the target folder.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The drawing must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/cam_profile.dxf. It requires two layers: 'PROFILE' (White) and 'BORE' (Cyan). The PROFILE layer contains a pear shape made from a base circle (radius 30 at 0,0) and a nose circle (radius 10 at 0,50) connected by tangent lines. The BORE layer contains a central hole (radius 10 at 0,0) with a keyway notch at the top (5mm wide, top edge at Y=13). Both shapes must be trimmed into single continuous outlines.
